displayTitle: Echo to Evidence: Evaluate and Report

beats.narration: Finding the signal is the easy half. Now decide what it is, whether it's recordable, and write a report that stands for years.

payload.title: ECHO TO EVIDENCE
payload.sub: Deciding what the signal is — and proving it on paper.

beats.narration: These words carry legal weight: anything on screen is an indication; a real feature, a discontinuity. Only failing acceptance criteria makes it a defect.
Recording is objective: record an echo reaching half of reference, and record anywhere the back wall drops below half, even without a new echo.

chips.s: Tilted laminations rob the back wall

beats.narration: Crossed the threshold? Peak the echo, slide until it halves, six dB, and the beam centerline sits over the edge. Trace the boundary.

payload.heading: Sizing: the 6 dB drop
payload.note: ECHO HALVES → CENTERLINE OVER THE EDGE

beats.narration: The report's test: another technician repeats your exam from paper alone. If it is not written down, it did not happen.

payload.sub: Repeatable from the paper alone — for years.
